fn is_cyclic_permutation(A : Array(Int64), B : Array(Int64)) : Bool
  k = A.size
  if k == 0
    return true
  end

  // B_double = B concatenated with itself
  // Since we are dealing with Int64 elements, dup and append are appropriate.
  B_double = B.dup.append(B)

  // Check all k possible starting points (rotations) in B_double
  for start in 0..<k
    match = true
    for i in 0..<k
      // Compare A[i] with the element in B_double starting at 'start'
      if A[i] != B_double[start + i]
        match = false
        break
      end
    end
    if match
      return true
    end
  end
  
  return false
end

def solve(nums : Array(Int64)) : Int64
  n = nums.size
  if n == 0
    return 0
  end

  // 1. Sort the original array to get the target array S
  // Sort requires comparison, using <=> on Int64
  S = nums.dup.sort { |a, b| a <=> b } 

  // 2. Find all divisors k of n
  // We use Int for loop iteration as N <= 10^5
  divisors = Array(Int64).new
  i = 1
  while i * i <= n
    if n % i == 0
      divisors.push(i.to_int64)
      if i * i != n
        divisors.push((n / i).to_int64)
      end
    end
    i += 1
  end

  total_sum = 0_int64

  // 3. Check each divisor k
  for k_int64 in divisors
    k = k_int64.to_i // k is small enough for standard Int type in Crystal
    
    is_sortable = true
    m = n / k // Number of blocks

    for i in 0..<m
      start_idx = i * k
      
      // Extract blocks B_i (from nums) and S_i (from S)
      // Slicing syntax: array[start_index .. end_index - 1]
      B_i = nums[start_idx .. start_idx + k - 1]
      S_i = S[start_idx .. start_idx + k - 1]
      
      // Check if S_i must be a cyclic permutation of B_i
      if !is_cyclic_permutation(S_i, B_i)
        is_sortable = false
        break
      end
    end

    if is_sortable
      total_sum += k_int64
    end
  end

  return total_sum
end

    k = a.size
    if k == 0
        return true

    # Concatenate B with itself (B_double). Length is 2k.
    b_double = b.dup.append(b)
